lines.text: Month 2. You've survived your first month in KL. Barely.
The salary notification comes in again. RM 2,500. Same number. But this month feels different — you know what's coming.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Month 2. How did Month 1 go?

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Honestly? I learned a lot.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: That's all Month 1 is for. The real test is whether you do anything differently with what you learned.
Aiden is quieter than usual when you get home. No Shopee tabs open. That's new.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: Hey... can I talk to you about something?

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: I kind of... overspent last month. Like, significantly. My credit card bill came in and I genuinely don't know how to pay it.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: RM 1,200. I thought I could handle it. I really thought I could.

choices.text: Offer to help him make a repayment plan.
Tell him to talk to Clara — she knows this stuff.
That's rough. But you warned him.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: You'd do that? I thought you'd just say 'I told you so'.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: That doesn't help anyone. Let's look at your expenses and figure out what you can cut this month.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: ...okay. Yeah. Let's do that.
You spend the next hour going through Aiden's spending. It's worse than you thought. But at least he's finally looking at it.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: Clara? She'll judge me.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: She went broke at 23. She won't judge you.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: ...really? I didn't know that.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: Yeah. You did. I should've listened.
There's an awkward silence. Aiden goes to his room. You feel like you said the right thing — but it didn't feel good.
Evelyn calls. She sounds more settled than last month.

lines.speaker: Evelyn
lines.text: Okay so — I actually tried the 50/30/20 thing this month. It was hard but I did it.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: How did it feel?

lines.speaker: Evelyn
lines.text: Honestly? Like I actually have control for the first time. I have RM 400 saved. It's not much but it's MINE and I put it there on purpose.

lines.speaker: Evelyn
lines.text: How about you? Did last month teach you anything?

choices.text: Yeah — I need to save more and spend less on wants.
I'm still figuring it out honestly.
I think I did okay. Could've saved more though.

lines.text: Clara finds you in the pantry during lunch. She has that look — the one that means she's about to say something you need to hear.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Can I show you something?

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: This is my savings tracker from when I was your age. I started it after I went broke. Every month, I wrote down what I saved and what I learned.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Month 1 was terrible. Month 2 was slightly less terrible. That's the whole secret — just keep going.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Did you ever feel like you were too far behind to catch up?

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Every single month for a year. And then one day I checked my account and realised I had three months of expenses saved. I cried in a public bathroom. It was not cute.
She laughs — actually laughs. You've never seen her do that before.
Salary day again. This time you open the banking app without hesitation. RM 2,500.
Last month taught you something. This month, you're applying it.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Month 2 budget time. You know the drill. Let's see if last month changed anything.
